title: Cytology in food animal practice.
author_string: Bohn, Andrea A, Callan, Robert J
journal: Vet Clin North Am Food Anim Pract
journal_full: The Veterinary clinics of North America. Food animal practice
abstract: Diagnostic cytology can greatly aid the clinician in determining a more refined diagnosis, prognosis, and treatment plan to serve the client and patient better. Sample collection is not difficult and can be done in the field as well as in a hospital setting. The collection and sample handling procedures described in this article can help the clinician to obtain diagnostically valuable samples. In many cases, preliminary cytologic evaluation can be performed by the general practitioner. Additional diagnostic evaluation and interpretation are readily available from trained pathologists at diagnostic laboratories.
